data.body:
Plan-review session: feedback triage → convergence design (epic star-lure-chaos) → step-budget exhaustion fix (PR #90) → observability + conflict resolution. Branches: main; feat/plan-review-calibration-and-verify-recovery (PR #88); worktree fix branch worktree-plan-review-agentic-budget (PR #90, off main).

## 1. Plan-review feedback triage (misinterpretation vs remediation)
Load-bearing finding: the critiqued BLOCK behavior was produced under an UNCOMMITTED config flip — 7 LLM criteria (F1,E2,G5,T4,T5e,COH,G6) set to blocking@0.7 — contradicting the documented advisory-v1 posture (only the DET floor blocks; calibration is post-implementation). So most "non-convergence/severity-incoherence" observations are the expected output of running LLM criteria as blockers pre-calibration, not gate design defects.
- REAL defects: (#1) non-determinism — Pass-1 finder + Pass-2 verifier run at provider-default temperature (NO temp/seed pinned in prod; only eval graders pin temp 0); verdict is deterministic only given stochastic findings. (#5) NO cross-criterion dedup of SURFACED findings (norm_id keys include criteria + is observability-only).
- Misinterpretations: (#4) "critical-yet-advisory is incoherent" — severity (impact) and the block decision (priority=validity×impact ≥ threshold + opted-in) are deliberately decoupled. (#6) "signed=True on BLOCK" — signing is strictly PASS-gated; the stale signal was a PRIOR PASS attestation on the ticket.
- Sharpened #6 into a real SOUNDNESS gap: a later BLOCK on UNCHANGED material does NOT revoke the prior PASS attestation, and the claim gate only checks "a certified, fresh, material-matching PASS exists" (not "latest verdict == PASS"). So a stale PASS survives a BLOCK → claimable-despite-BLOCK; non-determinism makes "same material, different verdict" reachable. Fix primitive: clear_signature (signing.py:413) on a non-PASS re-review, or have the claim gate require the latest verdict be PASS.

## 2. Shipped observability (PR #88 area + squashed-to-main calibration)
- Advisory cap DEFAULT_ADVISORY_CAP 10→20; CLI now surfaces overflow=N + a "(+N more …)" note (coverage.counts.advisory_overflow). docs/plan-review-gate.md updated.
- scripts/plan_review_impact_distribution.py — repeatable report over the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar corpus.
- discriminates_impact_levels eval scorer + {high_impact,low_impact} dataset pairs on plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml (guards severity discrimination; mirrors discriminates_true_from_false).
- EMPIRICAL: 245 REVIEW_RESULT events / 2,571 findings → impact ALREADY discriminates (15% critical, 40% none, mean 0.43; blast_radius 64% local, reversibility 74% easy). Refutes "everything critical"; impact calibration is a robustness item, NOT a blocker.

## 3. Convergent plan-edit re-review — epic star-lure-chaos (7d43-49c5-bc4d-4926), decomposed into 6 tasks
Chosen: FULL-run + DETERMINISTIC Pass-3 RISING FLOOR. Run all criteria every re-review (Pass-1 UNANCHORED → full recall, no sycophancy); drop only NOVEL low-priority findings; keep carryover + novel high-priority. NOVELTY scored in a SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call that alone sees prior findings (structural independence vs the verify.py:64 invariant); novelty score consumed deterministically in Pass-3 (mirrors validity). max-output-length/truncation → distinct non-retried error (user directive). Rejected: per-diff-region scoping; skip-clean-criteria reuse (UNSOUND — absence-of-finding reuse); whole-verdict probe.
- Freshness window = remediation-mode gate: default 60 min, reset on EACH review (full or partial), measured so a series of active edits stays in the loop; code-unchanged precondition; both-drifted → full review (drift_refresh untouched). Accepts UNBOUNDED within-session suppression of novel low-priority findings (user's deliberate convergence choice; bounded only by idle-lapse + carryover-still-enforced + sidecar-recording).
- Prereqs: sidecar _slim must persist finding TEXT (+suggested_fix/checklist_item) — currently dropped; discriminates_novelty eval as an EVIDENCE GATE before the drop rule is wired; defaults T_novel=0.7 + priority floor at the corpus p40 percentile.
- Plan review on the epic PASSed (signed); 12 advisories triaged + remediated into the epic + tasks S1/S2/S6. Note: remediation edits changed the material fingerprint → the signed PASS is now STALE-for-claim (re-run review-plan before claiming the epic or any child via the parent cascade).
- Gate robustness aside: a 6-child container bin packed into one agentic call hit a StructuredOutputError (the joe-debug subject below).

## 4. joe-debug: plan-review agentic step-budget exhaustion → PR #90 (branch worktree-plan-review-agentic-budget, off main)
Two DISTINCT failures the one warning conflated (both root-caused with deterministic experiments):
- RC-A ([plan-reviewer] hit step budget, the finder): framework default max_iterations=50 (~25 tool-call cycles) far too low; plan-review's Pass-1 never applied a per-op floor its sibling gates do (completion _VERIFY_MIN_STEPS=480, review/operations 120). FIX: DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS 50→250 (config.py) — safeguards every agentic caller; per-op floors still win via max(floor, configured) (review floor now no-ops since 250≥120; completion still floors to 480).
- RC-B ([plan-container] StructuredOutputError 196.8s, the container): (a) cfg.max_tokens was NEVER wired to the model — only cache flags were sent — so pydantic-ai fell back to its max_tokens=4096 default → a 6-child×2-criteria review truncates; (b) the structured-output retry re-ran the full agentic call 1+OUTPUT_RETRIES=3× before failing. FIX: wire cfg.max_tokens (16000, the non-streaming-safe ceiling) into model_settings for EVERY provider (runner.py); add UnretryableOutputError(StructuredOutputError) so truncation/max_tokens/length/refusal/content_filter FAST-FAIL (1 attempt) in _pai_structured while transient `error` stays retryable (errors.py, structured.py check_stop_reason).
- Tests: tests/unit/test_agentic_budget_and_truncation.py (new); updated test_review_budget.py, test_completion_budget.py, test_config_llm.py (defaults tuple), test_pydantic_ai_runner.py (max_tokens wired for all providers; cache flags anthropic-only). lint + mypy clean.
- CI fix (PR #90 first run red): test_config_llm::test_defaults and test_completion_budget still pinned the old 50 default → updated to 250 (substantive invariants preserved). Caused by running a targeted subset locally instead of the full suite before opening the PR — lesson: run the full suite (or at least the config/contracts tier) before pushing a default-changing edit.
- FOLLOW-UPS (not done): the container bin-packer (sizing.pack_container_bins) sizes bins by INPUT tokens only — no child-count cap, no output/step accounting — the deeper driver of the fat bin; an output/step-aware bin cap is the proper fix. True ">16K output" needs switching agentic calls to streaming (non-streaming cap ~16K per the Anthropic SDK).

## 5. PR #88 merge conflict resolved
Single conflict in src/rebar/llm/runner.py (the branch's verify-recovery vs main's #86 step-usage telemetry refactor). Took main's side — it extracts req_limit to a local that main's downstream telemetry (lines ~297/333) requires. Merged origin/main in, hooks (lint+mypy) passed, pushed; PR #88 now MERGEABLE.

## 6. Serena / tool-deferral (process)
Confirmed via claude-code-guide: hooks CANNOT un-defer MCP tool schemas (engine-side; SessionStart fires before MCP connect). ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH env controls deferral (default ~10% of context; "false" eager-loads the WHOLE surface = big per-turn tax; no per-server allowlist). Real low-friction fix = TRIM the MCP surface so Serena fits under the threshold (disable unused claude.ai connectors via /mcp). [User subsequently disconnected the unused connectors — 137 deferred tools removed this session.]
- Tool-choice lesson: prefer Serena symbol tools (find_symbol/find_referencing_symbols/get_symbols_overview) for Python symbol/reference navigation; reserve grep for literals/strings/JSON/YAML/docs; do NOT rely on negative grep evidence for load-bearing root-cause claims (a missing match ≠ no reference).

## Open items / state
- PR #90: MERGEABLE; CI re-running after the test-baseline fix.
- PR #88: MERGEABLE (BLOCKED only on branch-protection CI/review).
- Epic 7d43-49c5-bc4d-4926 (star-lure-chaos): decomposed (6 tasks), remediated; signed PASS is STALE-for-claim after remediation edits — re-run review-plan before claiming.
- Worktree env caveat: the fix worktree's .venv had to be hand-provisioned (make install did not pull [agents]/[dev]/mypy); the pre-commit hook needs the venv on PATH (activate in the same command as git commit) — worth fixing so the hook works out of the box.
